semanticCore: Walking, moving on foot, path, or slander
quranicRelevance: Walking/moving on foot ('masha'), and slander/tale-bearing (Mash-y - 'mash-sha'in binameem').

keyDerivations.meaning: To walk, go on foot, march, or proceed

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَشَّاءٍ بِنَمِيمٍ (68:11): A constant walker/spreader of slander (using walking to represent spreading gossip).
discardedNoise: Specific details of animal walking styles.
summaryEn: The root refers to walking and moving, depicting literal physical movement and the malicious 'walking' to spread gossip (Mash-sha').
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى المشي والحركة، واصفاً المشي المادي، والمشي المعنوي الخبيث لنقل النمائم وإفساد العلاقات (مشّاء).
rawText: أَمْشَىَ الدَّوَآءُ بَطْنَهُ 4 (A, K, art. حدر,) [The medicine moved, or purged, his bowels; made his belly to discharge itself:] and 🡻مَشَّى البَطْنَ. (TA, art. طوس, &c.) اِسْتَمْشَى بِالدَّوَآءِ 10 [He used the medicine as a laxative or purgative. (IbrD.)] (Az in L, art. عقر.) === اِسْتَمْشَى بِهِ, referring to a plant, (K in art. صع,) He drank its water (i. e. infusion or the like) for moving the bowels. (TA ibid.) تَمَاشَوْا 6 They walked, or went on foot, one towards, or to, another. (TA.) تَمَشَّى 5 i. q. مَشَى: (TA:) [or, properly, and accord. to general usage, he walked with slow steps: so I have rendered it voce دَلَفَ, &c.:] he walked heavily, with an effort. (TK voce تَزَحَّفَ.) [One says in the present day, خَرَجْتُ أَتَمَشَى I went forth taking a walk; and تَمَشَّى He walked; walked about.] === [Hence the saying,] تَمَشَّتْ فِيهِ حُمَيَّا الكَأْسِ [The intoxicating influence of the cup of wine pervaded him, or] crept in him. (TA.) See also تَفَشَّى. دَوَآءُ المَشِىِّ Medicine that moves, or purges, the bowels. (TA in art. طوس.) مَاشَاهُ 3 He walked, or went on foot, with him: he kept pace with him. See an ex. voce الأَحْصَّانِ. مَاشِيَةٌ A she-camel having numerous offspring. (S, Mgh.) === Hence, and مَوَاشٍ, as ominous of good, Camels, and cows, and sheep or goats that are for breeding and gain. (Mgh.) مَشَى 1 He walked, went, or went along; (MA, KL;) [in its primary sense] He went any pace upon his feet, afoot, or on foot; he footed; whether quickly or slowly: (Mgh, Msb:) he removed from place to place at pleasure: (Er-Rághib:) walked; went along, marched; travelled; trod; paced; stepped. See 5. === مَشَى also signifies He went on, or continued, in his course of action, &c. (Mughnee voce أَنْ, in explanation of this verb as used in Kur xxxviii. 5.) === [مَشَى † It (money) passed; was, or became, current. === † It (a calumny) was, or became, current. See مِئْبَرٌ.] === مَشَى بَنْطُهُ [His belly became moved, or in motion; it discharged itself.] (S, K, art. طلق; &c.) مَشَّآءٌ [That goes with energy; a good or strong goer;] strong to walk, or go, or go on foot. (TA voce رِجِيلٌ.) مَشَّىَ 2 see 4. مَمْشَى A passage, or way, by a place; (TA;) [a walking-place: the gangway of a ship?]
